_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Knee problems with pain and instability due to Varus knee malalignment, or early osteoarthritis confined to the medial compartment
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Knee problems in patients who have pain and instability because of a Varus knee malalignment
Surgery allows a postoperative valgus limb alignment with shifting the load-bearing axis of the lower limb laterally.
early osteoarthritis confined to medial compartment

chronic knee instability due to posterolateral corner (PLC), posterior cruciate ligament (PCL), and anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) Insufficiencies with concurrent malalignment in the coronal and/or sagittal plane.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with indication for surgery
Weightbearing (FLWB)/ Standing X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right)-affected limb justifying the indication

claim.label: Post op X-ray is labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) showing affected part.

cpd_claim.q: Did Post-operative X- ray shows the presence of implant?

common_queries: Clinical notes with indication for surgery missing or inadequate at pre-authorization.
Weightbearing (FLWB)/ Standing X-ray missing at pre-authorization, or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side.
Post-operation clinical photograph missing at claim submission.
Post-op X-ray missing at claim submission, or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side, or not showing affected part.
Post-operative X-ray does not show the presence of an implant.
Detailed operative note missing at claim submission.
Discharge summary missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: early osteoarthritis confined to medial compartment

chronic knee instability due to posterolateral corner (PLC), posterior cruciate ligament (PCL), and anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) Insufficiencies with concurrent malalignment in the coronal and/or sagittal plane.
